meta.title: Surface-Supplied Helium-Oxygen Decompression Table
meta.source: US Navy Diving Manual Rev 7, Chapter 12 (Surface-Supplied Mixed Gas), Table 12-4
meta.coverage_notes: Transcribed cell-by-cell from the official US Navy Rev 7 tables PDF page images pre-rendered in docs/tables/_render/ (pages 31-40), on 2026-07-04. Covers the full tabulated depth range 60-380 fsw in 10-fsw increments (33 depths), each with its printed bottom-time schedules (typically 10-120 min). Each stop carries its pre-baked gas phase by the printed column band: BOTTOM MIX (190-100 fsw stops) -> 50% O2 (90-40 fsw stops) -> 100% O2 (30/20 fsw stops). time_to_first_stop_min is the printed min:sec converted to decimal minutes; total_stop_min is the sum of the schedule's printed stop minutes. chamber_o2_periods stores the integer count printed in the last table column (the engine does not consume this field). max_o2_pct/min_o2_pct are the Max/Min O2% window printed for that depth and bound the bottom heliox mix only (not the 50%/100% deco gases). exceptional_exposure is set true for rows printed below an 'Exceptional Exposure' divider. Confidence is high for the shallow/mid depths (60-250 fsw, sparser grids) and good for the deep dense grids (260-380 fsw), though the deep multi-stop rows have many adjacent single-digit cells and should be spot-checked. Descent 75 fpm / ascent 30 fpm per the table header are recorded in meta but not separately modeled (not needed for a lookup tool). No repetitive-group / residual-nitrogen system exists for heliox -- repetitive_group is always None for this table's results, with a warning surfaced.
meta.unverified_warning: Transcribed from the official US Navy Rev 7 tables PDF (2026-07-04); spot-check against your manual before real use.
